name: The Hollowing
descriptionSeed: Where the Quickwood stops and the grey begins. This is the Ashwardens' edge — the near-march of the Palelands — and it is not a wasteland. It is TENDED. Things end here, correctly and on time, and the Ashwardens walk it with the same care the Rootkin walk their orchard. The two peoples are antipodes and they have a working border and a mutual, exhausted respect, and each thinks the other is making a category error about the nature of existence.
encounterFlavor: Ashwardens who are unhurried and kind and completely at peace with your mortality; things ending on schedule; something that is NOT ending on schedule, and that is the problem.
questSeeds: Something in the Hollowing is refusing to end, and the Ashwardens want help, and the Rootkin are pretending not to be delighted
A Rootkin has crossed into the Hollowing to save something, and that is, by treaty, a very serious act

encounterSeeds.hint: A precursor seal stands intact at the grey border, and its purpose-shadow reads PRISON

substrateSource.reason: The grey border holds a thin persistent charge — the seam between a drained wood and a tended one is not empty, it is under tension.
substrateSource.radiusNote: `radius` is in legacy map units; `radiusWorld` is RADII on the sphere and is the one mechanics should use. Chosen by sweep against the authored worldPos: at this scale all 26 sources resolve on the correct side of their region baseline and calibration drift stays ~0.05. Larger radii blanket a whole region (a source at ~55% strength everywhere inside it) and renormalization cancels the field flat — the failure CCode measured and reverted.

worldPosNote: The seam between drained wood and tended ground. A border, so inward of both poles it lies between. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
